site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 10 setup board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 10 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: official round 10 team lists are in and tonight starts the reset after a messy round 9
the mark hughes foundation ledger sits at 2 and 2 and minus $3.50 so this week should be about cleaner edges and smaller conviction
sharks head to head at around $2.25 now looks like the most interesting charity value number on the board
broncos have drifted because reynolds, mariner and staggs are out and manly get fogarty back
storm finally get jahrome hughes back but still carry a seven-game losing streak into sunday
the knights still look strong against the winless dragons with ponga running the show
no round 10 charity bet is locked yet

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: Sharks head to head leads the early charity shortlist
Cronulla have won their last five against South Sydney
The Rabbitohs lose Jack Wighton while the Sharks stay settled
At around $2.25 the price is finally worth paying attention to
Do not lock it until the market settles through late mail

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: The Bulldogs miss last week means the next bet should be smaller and cleaner
Storm still have the profile of a trap side even with Hughes back
Broncos versus Manly changed shape once Reynolds was ruled out again
Roosters are short but their injury list is getting longer
Do not confuse a juicy price with a good price

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: See whether Cronulla can back up the Tigers mauling against a stronger attack
Track if the Knights keep their momentum against a wounded Dragons side
Watch whether the Storm actually stabilise with Hughes back on deck
Keep Panthers in the reliability bucket until they finally give us a reason not to
Treat Round 10 as a reset, not a revenge week

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Sharks look like the most interesting value team on the slate
Sea Eagles get a big structural boost with Fogarty back
Cowboys still profile as one of the stronger home teams in the comp
The market may still be too slow to downgrade Melbourne properly

betCard.title: Round 10 Charity Bet
betCard.pick: Sharks head to head leads the shortlist
why: Cronulla come in settled after belting the Tigers and have won their last five against South Sydney.
The Rabbitohs lose Jack Wighton and have to reshuffle their backline and halves again.
At around $2.25, the Sharks finally offer a proper value number instead of another cramped favourite.
risks: Souths can still score points in bunches at Accor, especially with Latrell in this form.
Jye Gray returns and gives the Rabbitohs more spark through the middle third.
Cronulla are still a volatile side week to week, so this is value rather than safety.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.

watchlist: Whether Sharks stay value once the market reacts to Souths team changes.
How much the Broncos downgrade should matter without Reynolds and Staggs.
Whether Hughes changes enough for Melbourne to be trusted again.
Any signal that the next charity stake should stay at $5 rather than drifting back up.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 10 opens as a reset week, not a revenge week
lines: As of Thursday, 7 May 2026, official Round 10 team lists and late mail are in and the board can reset after a shaky Round 9.
The charity ledger is still sitting at 2-2 and -$3.50, so the right move this week is smaller, cleaner conviction.
There are better prices on the board than last week, but they still need discipline rather than emotion.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Sharks are the best value look, not the safest look
lines: Cronulla at around $2.25 is the first number in a while that looks like genuine charity value rather than just a fancy underdog.
South Sydney lose Jack Wighton and reshuffle, while the Sharks stay largely settled and bring strong head-to-head history into the match.
It is still a higher-variance play than a short favourite, so the price is doing the work here.
The right move is to keep the stake modest and only lock after one last late-mail check.

sections.title: Round 10 Fixtures
sections.heading: Known Round 10 slate after Team List Tuesday

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the Round 10 upgrade or downgrade
lines: Hot: Panthers, Sharks, Cowboys, and a healthier Knights side.
Cold: Storm, Bulldogs, and any faith that the Broncos are fully back without Reynolds.
The biggest swing price of the week is Sharks because the market still seems to respect Souths more than the lists do.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before locking Round 10
lines: The biggest late-mail signals are any movement around the Sharks-Rabbitohs edges, plus whether Ben Hunt sneaks closer for Brisbane.
Reynolds out again matters more than the raw Broncos price suggests, especially away at 4 Pines.
Storm getting Hughes back is meaningful, but not enough on its own to erase seven straight losses.
Keep the next charity stake controlled even if the value case feels better than last week.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
